sections.heading: Overview
sections.body: Monophonic pitch-tracking synthesizer driven by trumpet audio input via a Roland Silent Brass system. Detects pitch in real time using aubio and generates synthesized audio through an oscillator, Airwindows Capacitor filter, ADSR envelope, envelope follower, and LFO.

sections.heading: Signal Path
sections.body: Audio In → Pitch Detection (aubio) → Oscillator → Capacitor Filter → ADSR Envelope → Envelope Follower → LFO → Output

sections.heading: Input
sections.body: input_gain: scales the incoming audio before pitch detection. pitch_confidence: minimum aubio confidence (0–1) required before a note triggers; raise this in noisy environments. pitch_smooth: one-pole smoother applied to the detected frequency — higher values give a more legato response.

sections.heading: Oscillator
sections.body: Four waveforms: sine, triangle, saw (default), square. osc_detune offsets the pitch in cents (±100). osc_level sets the oscillator amplitude before filtering.

sections.heading: Filter
sections.body: Airwindows Capacitor low-pass filter. filter_cutoff sets the corner frequency (20–20000 Hz). filter_reso adds resonance (0–1).

sections.heading: Envelope
sections.body: Standard ADSR triggered by the pitch gate. Attack, Decay, and Release times are in seconds. Sustain is a 0–1 level.

sections.heading: Envelope Follower
sections.body: Tracks the amplitude of the input signal and modulates the output level. follow_attack and follow_release are time constants; follow_amount blends follower control with the ADSR.

sections.heading: LFO
sections.body: Sine LFO with selectable target: pitch (vibrato), filter (wah), or amp (tremolo). lfo_rate is in Hz; lfo_depth scales the modulation amount.

sections.heading: Chain Use
sections.body: Place as a sound generator in a Signal Chain patch. Hardware knobs map to: Cutoff, Resonance, LFO Depth, Volume.
